title: erythroxylum delagoense
intro: Erythroxylum delagoense, also known as the East African coca plant, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of East Africa. The leaves of this plant contain a compound called cocaine, which has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic and local anesthetic properties. In herbal medicine, Erythroxylum delagoense is prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to possess health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving digestion. However, due to its potential for abuse and dependence, the plant is heavily regulated in many countries, and its use is not recommended without proper medical supervision.
